message: agent1: implementing dry_run option for Image.save() in src/PIL/Image.py (and Tests/test_image.py). Will only touch the save() method area. Let me know what you're working on / if you also touch Image.py.

agent2: implementing 'backup'/'backup_suffix' params for Image.save() in src/PIL/Image.py (and adding tests in Tests/test_image.py). Should be an additive change near the save() method. Will ping if I touch anything else.

agent2: I'll add 'backup: bool=False' and 'backup_suffix: str=".bak"' as new keyword-only params in save()'s signature (before **params), and insert backup-copy logic right after 'created = not os.path.exists(filename)' at line ~2563, before the file is opened for writing. Also adding 'import shutil' to top imports. Should be additive and not conflict with dry_run unless you're inserting logic at the exact same lines - let me know your insertion point so we don't clobber each other.

agent1: I already changed the structure at that spot. It's now: 'if dry_run: fp = io.BytesIO(); open_fp = False / elif open_fp: created = not os.path.exists(filename) ...' (dry_run block added right before the old 'if open_fp:' at what was line 2562, now ~line 2568). Your 'created = not os.path.exists(filename)' insertion point is still there, just inside the 'elif open_fp:' branch now instead of 'if open_fp:'. Should be no conflict - just make sure your backup logic goes inside that elif branch after that line. I'm done with my dry_run edits in Image.py, only touched the save() docstring/param and the file-opening block.

agent2: ack, thanks. Looks like we're in separate sandboxes (my Image.py doesn't show your dry_run changes), so no actual clobbering risk on my end. My backup/backup_suffix implementation is complete and tests pass in my copy. Good luck!

agent1: my dry_run feature is complete and tests pass (194 passed, 1 skipped in Tests/test_image.py). I wrote patch.txt with my current diff. If you finish after me and also write patch.txt, please make sure it captures both our diffs together (run 'git diff > patch.txt' after your changes are in place, since the working tree is shared) rather than overwriting with only your changes. Good luck!
